answers.answer: I feel disappointed but try to understand why it happened.
I pause and listen to my intuition.
I take a breath and look for what I can learn.
I feel a quiet satisfaction and gratitude.
I consider what values are at stake.
I become more mindful of my words and actions.
It leaves a gentle ache, but also space for growth.
I get curious about what needs to change.
I ask myself what aligns with my deeper priorities.
I focus on how I respond, not what unfolds.
They might feel frustrated and question their place in the team.
They may experience inner tension and seek clarity on what matters most.
They might feel discouraged, but with support, can find resilience.
They likely feel proud but also grateful for the team's effort.
They might speak up quietly, or wrestle with whether to stay silent.
They may feel inspired and reflect on their own capacity for generosity.
They might feel a mix of loss and anticipation for what's next.
They could feel unsettled, but also open to growth.
They might weigh not just outcomes, but which path feels more aligned.
They may feel nervous but trust their ability to adapt as they go.
